summary: Official Windows service-family control for Bluetooth User Support Service. Microsoft documents the base service family, default start mode, and says it is OK to disable. The app disables instantiated per-user BluetoothUserService services through a wildcard service pattern.

setting.name: Bluetooth User Service
setting.casual_explanation: This per-user Bluetooth service supports Bluetooth features tied to each signed-in user session. Disabling it removes those per-user Bluetooth service behaviors.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it on systems where Bluetooth is never used and they want to strip down per-user Bluetooth services as well.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it reduces per-user Bluetooth service activity, but Bluetooth features relevant to each signed-in user session can stop working correctly.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Bluetooth User Service `(BluetoothUserService)` with a default start mode of `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`. Windows instantiates this as per-user services, so the app targets `BluetoothUserService_*` to disable created instances.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Per-user BluetoothUserService instances use Microsoft's documented Manual start mode.

allowed_values.label: Per-user Bluetooth service disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Instantiated BluetoothUserService per-user services are disabled until their start mode is restored.

targets.notes: Microsoft documents the base service family as BluetoothUserService. The app applies the setting to instantiated per-user service names using a wildcard.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables instantiated BluetoothUserService per-user services through the wildcard pattern BluetoothUserService_*.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems with the Bluetooth User Service family instantiated for signed-in users

states.rationale: Microsoft lists BluetoothUserService with a Manual default start mode.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves the documented per-user Bluetooth service behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems relying on Bluetooth features in user sessions
avoid_for: Systems intentionally removing Bluetooth support entirely

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode for instantiated BluetoothUserService instances.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want normal per-user Bluetooth behavior available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You never use Bluetooth and want these per-user services gone.
tradeoffs: Windows can still start the service family when user-session Bluetooth features need it.

recommended_profiles.label: No user Bluetooth services
intended_for: Stripped-down builds removing per-user Bluetooth components
avoid_for: Systems using Bluetooth features inside normal user sessions
recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally want per-user Bluetooth services disabled.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need Bluetooth features working for signed-in users.
tradeoffs: Bluetooth features tied to each user session can stop working correctly.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Bluetooth User Service
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Bluetooth User Support Service `(BluetoothUserService)` with start mode `Manual`, marks it `OK to disable`, and says it supports Bluetooth features relevant to each user session.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables instantiated `BluetoothUserService_*` services through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents Bluetooth User Support Service `(BluetoothUserService)` with a Manual default start mode, marks it `OK to disable`, and states that it supports Bluetooth features relevant to each user session. The app disables instantiated `BluetoothUserService_*` services, which is how the per-user service family appears in practice, so the runtime surface still matches the documented family cleanly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Bluetooth User Support Service (BluetoothUserService) | Manual | OK to disable | Supports proper functionality of Bluetooth features relevant to each user session.
validation_proof.notes: The official Learn services page documents the base per-user service family as BluetoothUserService. The app targets instantiated per-user services via BluetoothUserService_*.
